_comment: INVESTIGATION watchlist for the standing endpoint watcher (scripts/endpoint_watch.py, scheduled via .github/workflows/endpoint-watch.yml). These are NOT candidate feeds (those live in data/{stocking,access_points}/candidates.json and are auto-included by the watcher) -- they are one-time captures we want the moment a flaky/retired/down state GIS server becomes reachable again. Schema per entry: {id: stable slug; kind: one of field_dump|discover|verify; state: 2-letter code (for the in-state bbox check on verify/field_dump samples); url: the target (a layer URL for field_dump/verify, a folder/org root for discover); field: (field_dump only) a layer field name -- null means 'dump ALL fields + samples so a human can identify the one we need'; once the field is known, set it and the watcher additionally queries its distinct values; note: human context on what we're after and why}. kinds: field_dump -> probe a layer, and if up dump name/geometry/fields + 3 sample features (+ distinct values when field is set); discover -> if up, enumerate the folder's services / search the org and list trout/fish-named layers with record counts; verify -> run the candidate 4-check (meta, count, f=geojson sample, in-state bbox) on a url. The watcher never edits sources.json -- it only reports; promotion stays human-reviewed.

entries.note: Enumerate MD DNR Fisheries services for a wild-trout / special-management layer richer than DesignatedUse_Trout.
NDOW retired LCT_Occupied_Streams_NV (all layerIds 404). Find the republished Lahontan cutthroat occupied-streams service.
